source_quote: The study does not report zBMI (BMI z-scores or BMI standard deviation scores) as a continuous outcome. It reports raw BMI means and ordinal logistic regression ORs for shifts between BMI categories (normal, overweight, obesity). Table 3 reports ORs for the interaction TR*stage (treatment effect): overweight OR=0.89 (95%CI 0.73,1.11), obesity OR=0.68 (95%CI 0.51,0.91). No mean difference in zBMI is available.

After the intervention, there was no significant difference between the intervention and control groups in BMIsds at time 2 and 3. The change between time 2 and 1 in BMIsds was -0.11 in the intervention group, compared to -0.06 in the control group.

After the intervention, there was no significant difference between the intervention and control groups in BMIsds at time 2 and 3. No explicit point estimate or confidence interval is reported for the BMIsds comparison at time 3 (6-month follow-up). Table 4 shows prevalence data for weight categories at time 3 but not BMIsds means.
Table 4: BMI sds at T2: n=332, b=-0.03, p=0.46, 95% CI=-0.1 to 0.1. Results of Mixed Linear Regression adjusted for sex, parental education and baseline value.
Table 4: BMI sds at T3: n=318, b=0.013, p=0.79, 95% CI=-0.1 to 0.1. Results of Mixed Linear Regression adjusted for sex, parental education and baseline value.
